{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"Taconite is a geological substance defined by its mineralogical composition (iron minerals interlayered with quartz and chert), its percentage iron content (20–30%), and its origin in specific rock formations. These identity conditions require technical mineralogy and stratigraphy beyond the prime lexicon. The substance is tied to a particular geological epoch and geographic formation (Biwabik Iron Formation, Mesabi Range). It belongs to the earth-science sector, not this tier."}
